an10.76:1.1.pli: “Tayome, bhikkhave, dhammā loke na saṁvijjeyyuṁ, na tathāgato loke uppajjeyya arahaṁ sammāsambuddho, na tathāgatappavedito dhammavinayo loke dibbeyya.
an10.76:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, if three things were not found, the Realized One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha would not arise in the world, and the teaching and training proclaimed by the Realized One would not shine in the world.

an10.76:1.4.pli: ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo dhammā loke na saṁvijjeyyuṁ, na tathāgato loke uppajjeyya arahaṁ sammāsambuddho, na tathāgatappavedito dhammavinayo loke dibbeyya.
an10.76:1.4.eng: If these three things were not found, the Realized One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha would not arise in the world, and the teaching and training proclaimed by the Realized One would not shine in the world.

an10.76:1.5.pli: Yasmā ca kho, bhikkhave, ime tayo dhammā loke saṁvijjanti tasmā tathāgato loke uppajjati arahaṁ sammāsambuddho, tasmā tathāgatappavedito dhammavinayo loke dibbati.
an10.76:1.5.eng: But since these three things are found, the Realized One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha arises in the world, and the teaching and training proclaimed by the Realized One shines in the world.

an10.76:2.1.pli: Tayome, bhikkhave, dhamme appahāya abhabbo jātiṁ pahātuṁ jaraṁ pahātuṁ maraṇaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:2.1.eng: Without giving up three things you can’t give up rebirth, old age, and death.

an10.76:2.3.pli: Rāgaṁ appahāya, dosaṁ appahāya, mohaṁ appahāya—

an10.76:2.4.pli: ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo dhamme appahāya abhabbo jātiṁ pahātuṁ jaraṁ pahātuṁ maraṇaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:2.4.eng: Without giving up these three things you can’t give up rebirth, old age, and death.

an10.76:3.1.pli: Tayome, bhikkhave, dhamme appahāya abhabbo rāgaṁ pahātuṁ dosaṁ pahātuṁ mohaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:3.1.eng: Without giving up three things you can’t give up greed, hate, and delusion.

an10.76:3.3.pli: Sakkāyadiṭṭhiṁ appahāya, vicikicchaṁ appahāya, sīlabbataparāmāsaṁ appahāya—
an10.76:3.3.eng: Substantialist view, doubt, and misapprehension of precepts and observances.

an10.76:3.4.pli: ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo dhamme appahāya abhabbo rāgaṁ pahātuṁ dosaṁ pahātuṁ mohaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:3.4.eng: Without giving up these three things you can’t give up greed, hate, and delusion.

an10.76:4.1.pli: Tayome, bhikkhave, dhamme appahāya abhabbo sakkāyadiṭṭhiṁ pahātuṁ vicikicchaṁ pahātuṁ sīlabbataparāmāsaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:4.1.eng: Without giving up three things you can’t give up substantialist view, doubt, and misapprehension of precepts and observances.

an10.76:4.3.pli: Ayonisomanasikāraṁ appahāya, kummaggasevanaṁ appahāya, cetaso līnattaṁ appahāya—
an10.76:4.3.eng: Irrational application of mind, following a wrong path, and mental sluggishness.

an10.76:4.4.pli: ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo dhamme appahāya abhabbo sakkāyadiṭṭhiṁ pahātuṁ vicikicchaṁ pahātuṁ sīlabbataparāmāsaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:4.4.eng: Without giving up these three things you can’t give up substantialist view, doubt, and misapprehension of precepts and observances.

an10.76:5.1.pli: Tayome, bhikkhave, dhamme appahāya abhabbo ayoniso manasikāraṁ pahātuṁ kummaggasevanaṁ pahātuṁ cetaso līnattaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:5.1.eng: Without giving up three things you can’t give up irrational application of mind, following a wrong path, and mental sluggishness.

an10.76:5.3.pli: Muṭṭhassaccaṁ appahāya, asampajaññaṁ appahāya, cetaso vikkhepaṁ appahāya—
an10.76:5.3.eng: Unmindfulness, lack of situational awareness, and scattered mind.

an10.76:5.4.pli: ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo dhamme appahāya abhabbo ayonisomanasikāraṁ pahātuṁ kummaggasevanaṁ pahātuṁ cetaso līnattaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:5.4.eng: Without giving up these three things you can’t give up irrational application of mind, following a wrong path, and mental sluggishness.

an10.76:6.1.pli: Tayome, bhikkhave, dhamme appahāya abhabbo muṭṭhassaccaṁ pahātuṁ asampajaññaṁ pahātuṁ cetaso vikkhepaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:6.1.eng: Without giving up three things you can’t give up unmindfulness, lack of situational awareness, and scattered mind.

an10.76:6.3.pli: Ariyānaṁ adassanakamyataṁ appahāya, ariyadhammassa asotukamyataṁ appahāya, upārambhacittataṁ appahāya—
an10.76:6.3.eng: Not wanting to see the noble ones, not wanting to hear the teaching of the noble ones, and a fault-finding mind.

an10.76:6.4.pli: ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo dhamme appahāya abhabbo muṭṭhassaccaṁ pahātuṁ asampajaññaṁ pahātuṁ cetaso vikkhepaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:6.4.eng: Without giving up these three things you can’t give up unmindfulness, lack of situational awareness, and scattered mind.

an10.76:7.1.pli: Tayome, bhikkhave, dhamme appahāya abhabbo ariyānaṁ adassanakamyataṁ pahātuṁ ariyadhammassa asotukamyataṁ pahātuṁ upārambhacittataṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:7.1.eng: Without giving up three things you can’t give up not wanting to see the noble ones, not wanting to hear the teaching of the noble ones, and a fault-finding mind.

an10.76:7.3.pli: Uddhaccaṁ appahāya, asaṁvaraṁ appahāya, dussilyaṁ appahāya—
an10.76:7.3.eng: Restlessness, lack of restraint, and unethical conduct.

an10.76:7.4.pli: ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo dhamme appahāya abhabbo ariyānaṁ adassanakamyataṁ pahātuṁ ariyadhammassa asotukamyataṁ pahātuṁ upārambhacittataṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:7.4.eng: Without giving up these three things you can’t give up not wanting to see the noble ones, not wanting to hear the teaching of the noble ones, and a fault-finding mind.

an10.76:8.1.pli: Tayome, bhikkhave, dhamme appahāya abhabbo uddhaccaṁ pahātuṁ asaṁvaraṁ pahātuṁ dussilyaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:8.1.eng: Without giving up three things you can’t give up restlessness, lack of restraint, and unethical conduct.

an10.76:8.3.pli: Assaddhiyaṁ appahāya, avadaññutaṁ appahāya, kosajjaṁ appahāya—

an10.76:8.4.pli: ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo dhamme appahāya abhabbo uddhaccaṁ pahātuṁ asaṁvaraṁ pahātuṁ dussilyaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:8.4.eng: Without giving up these three things you can’t give up restlessness, lack of restraint, and unethical conduct.

an10.76:9.1.pli: Tayome, bhikkhave, dhamme appahāya abhabbo assaddhiyaṁ pahātuṁ avadaññutaṁ pahātuṁ kosajjaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:9.1.eng: Without giving up three things you can’t give up faithlessness, uncharitableness, and laziness.

an10.76:9.3.pli: Anādariyaṁ appahāya, dovacassataṁ appahāya, pāpamittataṁ appahāya—
an10.76:9.3.eng: Disregard, being hard to admonish, and having bad friends.

an10.76:9.4.pli: ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo dhamme appahāya abhabbo assaddhiyaṁ pahātuṁ avadaññutaṁ pahātuṁ kosajjaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:9.4.eng: Without giving up these three things you can’t give up faithlessness, uncharitableness, and laziness.

an10.76:10.1.pli: Tayome, bhikkhave, dhamme appahāya abhabbo anādariyaṁ pahātuṁ dovacassataṁ pahātuṁ pāpamittataṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:10.1.eng: Without giving up three things you can’t give up disregard, being hard to admonish, and having bad friends.

an10.76:10.3.pli: Ahirikaṁ appahāya, anottappaṁ appahāya, pamādaṁ appahāya—
an10.76:10.3.eng: Lack of conscience, imprudence, and negligence.

an10.76:10.4.pli: ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo dhamme appahāya abhabbo anādariyaṁ pahātuṁ dovacassataṁ pahātuṁ pāpamittataṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:10.4.eng: Without giving up these three things you can’t give up disregard, being hard to admonish, and having bad friends.

an10.76:11.1.eng: Mendicants, someone who lacks conscience and prudence is negligent.

an10.76:11.2.pli: So pamatto samāno abhabbo anādariyaṁ pahātuṁ dovacassataṁ pahātuṁ pāpamittataṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:11.2.eng: When you’re negligent you can’t give up disregard, being hard to admonish, and having bad friends.

an10.76:11.3.pli: So pāpamitto samāno abhabbo assaddhiyaṁ pahātuṁ avadaññutaṁ pahātuṁ kosajjaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:11.3.eng: When you’ve got bad friends you can’t give up faithlessness, uncharitableness, and laziness.

an10.76:11.4.pli: So kusīto samāno abhabbo uddhaccaṁ pahātuṁ asaṁvaraṁ pahātuṁ dussilyaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:11.4.eng: When you’re lazy you can’t give up restlessness, lack of restraint, and unethical conduct.

an10.76:11.5.pli: So dussīlo samāno abhabbo ariyānaṁ adassanakamyataṁ pahātuṁ ariyadhammassa asotukamyataṁ pahātuṁ upārambhacittataṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:11.5.eng: When you’re unethical you can’t give up not wanting to see the noble ones, not wanting to hear the teaching of the noble ones, and a fault-finding mind.

an10.76:11.6.pli: So upārambhacitto samāno abhabbo muṭṭhassaccaṁ pahātuṁ asampajaññaṁ pahātuṁ cetaso vikkhepaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:11.6.eng: When you’ve got a fault-finding mind you can’t give up unmindfulness, lack of situational awareness, and a scattered mind.

an10.76:11.7.pli: So vikkhittacitto samāno abhabbo ayonisomanasikāraṁ pahātuṁ kummaggasevanaṁ pahātuṁ cetaso līnattaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:11.7.eng: When your mind is scattered you can’t give up irrational application of mind, following a wrong path, and mental sluggishness.

an10.76:11.8.pli: So līnacitto samāno abhabbo sakkāyadiṭṭhiṁ pahātuṁ vicikicchaṁ pahātuṁ sīlabbataparāmāsaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:11.8.eng: When your mind is sluggish you can’t give up substantialist view, doubt, and misapprehension of precepts and observances.

an10.76:11.9.pli: So vicikiccho samāno abhabbo rāgaṁ pahātuṁ dosaṁ pahātuṁ mohaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:11.9.eng: When you have doubts you can’t give up greed, hate, and delusion.

an10.76:11.10.pli: So rāgaṁ appahāya dosaṁ appahāya mohaṁ appahāya abhabbo jātiṁ pahātuṁ jaraṁ pahātuṁ maraṇaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:11.10.eng: Without giving up greed, hate, and delusion you can’t give up rebirth, old age, and death.

an10.76:12.1.pli: Tayome, bhikkhave, dhamme pahāya bhabbo jātiṁ pahātuṁ jaraṁ pahātuṁ maraṇaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:12.1.eng: After giving up three things you can give up rebirth, old age, and death.

an10.76:12.3.pli: Rāgaṁ pahāya, dosaṁ pahāya, mohaṁ pahāya—

an10.76:12.4.pli: ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo dhamme pahāya bhabbo jātiṁ pahātuṁ jaraṁ pahātuṁ maraṇaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:12.4.eng: After giving up these three things you can give up rebirth, old age, and death.

an10.76:13.1.pli: Tayome, bhikkhave, dhamme pahāya bhabbo rāgaṁ pahātuṁ dosaṁ pahātuṁ mohaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:13.1.eng: After giving up three things you can give up greed, hate, and delusion.

an10.76:13.3.pli: Sakkāyadiṭṭhiṁ pahāya, vicikicchaṁ pahāya, sīlabbataparāmāsaṁ pahāya—
an10.76:13.3.eng: Substantialist view, doubt, and misapprehension of precepts and observances.

an10.76:13.4.pli: ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo dhamme pahāya bhabbo rāgaṁ pahātuṁ dosaṁ pahātuṁ mohaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:13.4.eng: After giving up these three things you can give up greed, hate, and delusion.

an10.76:14.1.pli: Tayome, bhikkhave, dhamme pahāya bhabbo sakkāyadiṭṭhiṁ pahātuṁ vicikicchaṁ pahātuṁ sīlabbataparāmāsaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:14.1.eng: After giving up three things you can give up substantialist view, doubt, and misapprehension of precepts and observances.

an10.76:14.3.pli: Ayonisomanasikāraṁ pahāya, kummaggasevanaṁ pahāya, cetaso līnattaṁ pahāya—
an10.76:14.3.eng: Irrational application of mind, following a wrong path, and mental sluggishness.

an10.76:14.4.pli: ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo dhamme pahāya bhabbo sakkāyadiṭṭhiṁ pahātuṁ vicikicchaṁ pahātuṁ sīlabbataparāmāsaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:14.4.eng: After giving up these three things you can give up substantialist view, doubt, and misapprehension of precepts and observances.

an10.76:15.1.pli: Tayome, bhikkhave, dhamme pahāya bhabbo ayonisomanasikāraṁ pahātuṁ kummaggasevanaṁ pahātuṁ cetaso līnattaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:15.1.eng: After giving up three things you can give up irrational application of mind, following a wrong path, and mental sluggishness.

an10.76:15.3.pli: Muṭṭhassaccaṁ pahāya, asampajaññaṁ pahāya, cetaso vikkhepaṁ pahāya—
an10.76:15.3.eng: Unmindfulness, lack of situational awareness, and a scattered mind.

an10.76:15.4.pli: ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo dhamme pahāya bhabbo ayonisomanasikāraṁ pahātuṁ kummaggasevanaṁ pahātuṁ cetaso līnattaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:15.4.eng: After giving up these three things you can give up irrational application of mind, following a wrong path, and mental sluggishness.

an10.76:16.1.pli: Tayome, bhikkhave, dhamme pahāya bhabbo muṭṭhassaccaṁ pahātuṁ asampajaññaṁ pahātuṁ cetaso vikkhepaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:16.1.eng: After giving up three things you can give up unmindfulness, lack of situational awareness, and scattered mind.

an10.76:16.3.pli: Ariyānaṁ adassanakamyataṁ pahāya, ariyadhammassa asotukamyataṁ pahāya, upārambhacittataṁ pahāya—
an10.76:16.3.eng: Not wanting to see the noble ones, not wanting to hear the teaching of the noble ones, and a fault-finding mind.

an10.76:16.4.pli: ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo dhamme pahāya bhabbo muṭṭhassaccaṁ pahātuṁ asampajaññaṁ pahātuṁ cetaso vikkhepaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:16.4.eng: After giving up these three things you can give up unmindfulness, lack of situational awareness, and scattered mind.

an10.76:17.1.pli: Tayome, bhikkhave, dhamme pahāya bhabbo ariyānaṁ adassanakamyataṁ pahātuṁ ariyadhammassa asotukamyataṁ pahātuṁ upārambhacittataṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:17.1.eng: After giving up three things you can give up not wanting to see the noble ones, not wanting to hear the teaching of the noble ones, and a fault-finding mind.

an10.76:17.3.pli: Uddhaccaṁ pahāya, asaṁvaraṁ pahāya, dussilyaṁ pahāya—
an10.76:17.3.eng: Restlessness, lack of restraint, and unethical conduct.

an10.76:17.4.pli: ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo dhamme pahāya bhabbo ariyānaṁ adassanakamyataṁ pahātuṁ ariyadhammassa asotukamyataṁ pahātuṁ upārambhacittataṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:17.4.eng: After giving up these three things you can give up not wanting to see the noble ones, not wanting to hear the teaching of the noble ones, and a fault-finding mind.

an10.76:18.1.pli: Tayome, bhikkhave, dhamme pahāya bhabbo uddhaccaṁ pahātuṁ asaṁvaraṁ pahātuṁ dussilyaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:18.1.eng: After giving up three things you can give up restlessness, lack of restraint, and unethical conduct.

an10.76:18.3.pli: Assaddhiyaṁ pahāya, avadaññutaṁ pahāya, kosajjaṁ pahāya—

an10.76:18.4.pli: ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo dhamme pahāya bhabbo uddhaccaṁ pahātuṁ asaṁvaraṁ pahātuṁ dussilyaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:18.4.eng: After giving up these three things you can give up restlessness, lack of restraint, and unethical conduct.

an10.76:19.1.pli: Tayome, bhikkhave, dhamme pahāya bhabbo assaddhiyaṁ pahātuṁ avadaññutaṁ pahātuṁ kosajjaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:19.1.eng: After giving up three things you can give up faithlessness, uncharitableness, and laziness.

an10.76:19.3.pli: Anādariyaṁ pahāya, dovacassataṁ pahāya, pāpamittataṁ pahāya—
an10.76:19.3.eng: Disregard, being hard to admonish, and having bad friends.

an10.76:19.4.pli: ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo dhamme pahāya bhabbo assaddhiyaṁ pahātuṁ avadaññutaṁ pahātuṁ kosajjaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:19.4.eng: After giving up these three things you can give up faithlessness, uncharitableness, and laziness.

an10.76:20.1.pli: Tayome, bhikkhave, dhamme pahāya bhabbo anādariyaṁ pahātuṁ dovacassataṁ pahātuṁ pāpamittataṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:20.1.eng: After giving up three things you can give up disregard, being hard to admonish, and having bad friends.

an10.76:20.3.pli: Ahirikaṁ pahāya, anottappaṁ pahāya, pamādaṁ pahāya—
an10.76:20.3.eng: Lack of conscience, imprudence, and negligence.

an10.76:20.4.pli: ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo dhamme pahāya bhabbo anādariyaṁ pahātuṁ dovacassataṁ pahātuṁ pāpamittataṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:20.4.eng: After giving up these three things you can give up disregard, being hard to admonish, and having bad friends.

an10.76:21.1.eng: Mendicants, someone who has conscience and prudence is diligent.

an10.76:21.2.pli: So appamatto samāno bhabbo anādariyaṁ pahātuṁ dovacassataṁ pahātuṁ pāpamittataṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:21.2.eng: When you’re diligent you can give up disregard, being hard to admonish, and having bad friends.

an10.76:21.3.pli: So kalyāṇamitto samāno bhabbo assaddhiyaṁ pahātuṁ avadaññutaṁ pahātuṁ kosajjaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:21.3.eng: When you’ve got good friends you can give up faithlessness, uncharitableness, and laziness.

an10.76:21.4.pli: So āraddhavīriyo samāno bhabbo uddhaccaṁ pahātuṁ asaṁvaraṁ pahātuṁ dussilyaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:21.4.eng: When you’re energetic you can give up restlessness, lack of restraint, and unethical conduct.

an10.76:21.5.pli: So sīlavā samāno bhabbo ariyānaṁ adassanakamyataṁ pahātuṁ ariyadhammassa asotukamyataṁ pahātuṁ upārambhacittataṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:21.5.eng: When you’re ethical you can give up not wanting to see the noble ones, not wanting to hear the teaching of the noble ones, and a fault-finding mind.

an10.76:21.6.pli: So anupārambhacitto samāno bhabbo muṭṭhassaccaṁ pahātuṁ asampajaññaṁ pahātuṁ cetaso vikkhepaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:21.6.eng: When you don’t have a fault-finding mind you can give up unmindfulness, lack of situational awareness, and a scattered mind.

an10.76:21.7.pli: So avikkhittacitto samāno bhabbo ayonisomanasikāraṁ pahātuṁ kummaggasevanaṁ pahātuṁ cetaso līnattaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:21.7.eng: When your mind isn’t scattered you can give up irrational application of mind, following a wrong path, and mental sluggishness.

an10.76:21.8.pli: So alīnacitto samāno bhabbo sakkāyadiṭṭhiṁ pahātuṁ vicikicchaṁ pahātuṁ sīlabbataparāmāsaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:21.8.eng: When your mind isn’t sluggish you can give up substantialist view, doubt, and misapprehension of precepts and observances.

an10.76:21.9.pli: So avicikiccho samāno bhabbo rāgaṁ pahātuṁ dosaṁ pahātuṁ mohaṁ pahātuṁ.
an10.76:21.9.eng: When you have no doubts you can give up greed, hate, and delusion.

an10.76:21.10.pli: So rāgaṁ pahāya dosaṁ pahāya mohaṁ pahāya bhabbo jātiṁ pahātuṁ jaraṁ pahātuṁ maraṇaṁ pahātun”ti.
an10.76:21.10.eng: After giving up greed, hate, and delusion you can give up rebirth, old age, and death.”

an10.77:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, a crow has ten bad qualities.

an10.77:1.3.pli: Dhaṁsī ca, pagabbho ca, tintiṇo ca, mahagghaso ca, luddo ca, akāruṇiko ca, dubbalo ca, oravitā ca, muṭṭhassati ca, necayiko ca—
an10.77:1.3.eng: They’re rude and impudent, gluttonous and voracious, cruel and pitiless, weak and raucous, unmindful and acquisitive.

an10.77:1.4.pli: imehi kho, bhikkhave, dasahi asaddhammehi samannāgato kāko.
an10.77:1.4.eng: A crow possesses these ten bad qualities.

an10.77:1.5.pli: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, dasahi asaddhammehi samannāgato pāpabhikkhu.
an10.77:1.5.eng: In the same way, a bad mendicant possesses these ten bad qualities.

an10.77:1.7.pli: Dhaṁsī ca, pagabbho ca, tintiṇo ca, mahagghaso ca, luddo ca, akāruṇiko ca, dubbalo ca, oravitā ca, muṭṭhassati ca, necayiko ca—
an10.77:1.7.eng: They’re rude and impudent, gluttonous and voracious, cruel and pitiless, weak and raucous, unmindful and acquisitive.

an10.77:1.8.pli: imehi kho, bhikkhave, dasahi asaddhammehi samannāgato pāpabhikkhū”ti.
an10.77:1.8.eng: A bad mendicant possesses these ten bad qualities.”

an10.78:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, Jain ascetics have ten bad qualities.

an10.78:1.8.eng: They glorify themselves and put others down.

an10.78:1.9.eng: They’re attached to their own views, holding them tight, and refusing to let go.

an10.78:1.13.pli: imehi kho, bhikkhave, dasahi asaddhammehi samannāgatā nigaṇṭhā”ti.
an10.78:1.13.eng: Jain ascetics possess these ten bad qualities.”

an10.79:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, there are ten grounds for resentment.

an10.79:1.3.eng: Thinking: ‘They did wrong to me,’ you harbor resentment.

an10.79:1.4.eng: Thinking: ‘They are doing wrong to me’ …

an10.79:1.6.eng: ‘They did wrong by someone I love’ …

an10.79:1.7.eng: ‘They are doing wrong by someone I love’ …

an10.79:1.8.eng: ‘They will do wrong by someone I love’ …

an10.79:1.11.eng: Thinking: ‘They will help someone I dislike,’ you harbor resentment.

an10.79:1.12.eng: You get angry for no reason.

an10.79:1.13.eng: These are the ten grounds for resentment.”

an10.8:1.1.pli: “Saddho ca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu hoti, no ca sīlavā;
an10.8:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, a mendicant is faithful but not ethical.

an10.8:1.2.eng: So they’re incomplete in that respect,

an10.8:1.4.eng: ‘How can I become faithful and ethical?’

an10.8:1.5.pli: Yato ca kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu saddho ca hoti sīlavā ca, evaṁ so tenaṅgena paripūro hoti.
an10.8:1.5.eng: When the mendicant is faithful and ethical, they’re complete in that respect.

an10.8:2.1.pli: Saddho ca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu hoti sīlavā ca, no ca bahussuto …pe…
an10.8:2.1.eng: A mendicant is faithful and ethical, but not learned. …

an10.8:2.4.pli: parisāvacaro ca, no ca visārado parisāya dhammaṁ deseti …
an10.8:2.4.eng: they don’t teach Dhamma to the assembly with assurance …

an10.8:2.5.pli: visārado ca parisāya dhammaṁ deseti, no ca vinayadharo …
an10.8:2.5.eng: they’re not an expert in the monastic law …

an10.8:2.6.pli: vinayadharo ca, no ca āraññiko pantasenāsano …
an10.8:2.6.eng: they don’t stay in the wilderness, in remote lodgings …

an10.8:2.7.pli: āraññiko ca pantasenāsano, no ca catunnaṁ jhānānaṁ ābhicetasikānaṁ diṭṭhadhammasukhavihārānaṁ nikāmalābhī hoti akicchalābhī akasiralābhī …
an10.8:2.7.eng: they don’t get the four absorptions—blissful meditations in this life that belong to the higher mind—when they want, without trouble or difficulty …

an10.8:2.8.pli: catunnañca jhānānaṁ ābhicetasikānaṁ diṭṭhadhammasukhavihārānaṁ nikāmalābhī hoti akicchalābhī akasiralābhī, no ca āsavānaṁ khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati.
an10.8:2.8.eng: they don’t realize the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life, and live having realized it with their own insight due to the ending of defilements.

an10.8:2.9.eng: So they’re incomplete in that respect,

an10.8:2.11.pli: ‘kintāhaṁ saddho ca assaṁ, sīlavā ca, bahussuto ca, dhammakathiko ca, parisāvacaro ca, visārado ca parisāya dhammaṁ deseyyaṁ, vinayadharo ca, āraññiko ca pantasenāsano, catunnañca jhānānaṁ ābhicetasikānaṁ diṭṭhadhammasukhavihārānaṁ nikāmalābhī assaṁ akicchalābhī akasiralābhī, āsavānañca khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja vihareyyan’ti.
an10.8:2.11.eng: ‘How can I become faithful, ethical, and learned, a Dhamma speaker, one who frequents assemblies, one who teaches Dhamma to the assembly with assurance, an expert in the training, one who lives in the wilderness, in remote lodgings, one who gets the four absorptions when they want, and one who lives having realized the ending of defilements?’

an10.8:3.1.pli: Yato ca kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu saddho ca hoti, sīlavā ca, bahussuto ca, dhammakathiko ca, parisāvacaro ca, visārado ca parisāya dhammaṁ deseti, vinayadharo ca, āraññiko ca pantasenāsano, catunnañca jhānānaṁ ābhicetasikānaṁ diṭṭhadhammasukhavihārānaṁ nikāmalābhī hoti akicchalābhī akasiralābhī, āsavānañca khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati;
an10.8:3.1.eng: When they’re faithful, ethical, and learned, a Dhamma speaker, one who frequents assemblies, one who teaches Dhamma to the assembly with assurance, an expert in the training, one who lives in the wilderness, in remote lodgings, one who gets the four absorptions when they want, and one who lives having realized the ending of defilements,

an10.8:3.3.pli: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, dasahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu samantapāsādiko ca hoti sabbākāraparipūro cā”ti.
an10.8:3.3.eng: A mendicant who possesses these ten qualities is impressive all around, and is complete in every respect.”

an10.80:1.1.eng: “Mendicants, there are these ten ways to get rid of resentment.

an10.80:1.3.pli: ‘Anatthaṁ me acari, taṁ kutettha labbhā’ti āghātaṁ paṭivineti,
an10.80:1.3.eng: Thinking: ‘They harmed me, but what can I possibly do?’ you get rid of resentment.

an10.80:1.4.pli: ‘anatthaṁ me carati, taṁ kutettha labbhā’ti āghātaṁ paṭivineti,

an10.80:1.5.pli: ‘anatthaṁ me carissati, taṁ kutettha labbhā’ti āghātaṁ paṭivineti,

an10.80:1.8.pli: carissati, taṁ kutettha labbhāti āghātaṁ paṭivineti, appiyassa me amanāpassa atthaṁ acari …pe…
an10.80:1.8.eng: ‘They will harm someone I love …’ … They helped someone I dislike …’ …

an10.80:1.10.pli: atthaṁ carissati, taṁ kutettha labbhāti āghātaṁ paṭivineti,
an10.80:1.10.eng: Thinking: ‘They will help someone I dislike, but what can I possibly do?’ you get rid of resentment.

an10.80:1.11.eng: And you don’t get angry for no reason.

an10.80:1.12.eng: These are the ten ways of getting rid of resentment.”

an10.81:1.1.pli: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā campāyaṁ viharati gaggarāya pokkharaṇiyā tīre.
an10.81:1.1.eng: At one time the Buddha was staying near Campā on the banks of the Gaggarā Lotus Pond.

an10.81:1.2.pli: Atha kho āyasmā vāhano yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā vāhano bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
an10.81:1.2.eng: Then Venerable Bāhuna went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:

an10.81:1.3.pli: “katihi nu kho, bhante, dhammehi tathāgato nissaṭo visaṁyutto vippamutto vimariyādīkatena cetasā viharatī”ti?
an10.81:1.3.eng: “Sir, how many things has the Realized One escaped from, so that he lives detached, liberated, with mind free of boundaries?”

an10.81:2.1.pli: “Dasahi kho, vāhana, dhammehi tathāgato nissaṭo visaṁyutto vippamutto vimariyādīkatena cetasā viharati.
an10.81:2.1.eng: “Bāhuna, the Realized One has escaped from ten things, so that he lives detached, liberated, with mind free of boundaries.

an10.81:2.3.pli: Rūpena kho, vāhana, tathāgato nissaṭo visaṁyutto vippamutto vimariyādīkatena cetasā viharati,

an10.81:2.12.pli: kilesehi kho, vāhana, tathāgato nissaṭo visaṁyutto vippamutto vimariyādīkatena cetasā viharati.

an10.81:2.13.pli: Seyyathāpi, vāhana, uppalaṁ vā padumaṁ vā puṇḍarīkaṁ vā udake jātaṁ udake saṁvaḍḍhaṁ udakā paccuggamma ṭhitaṁ anupalittaṁ udakena;
an10.81:2.13.eng: Suppose there was a blue water lily, or a pink or white lotus. Though it sprouted and grew in the water, it would rise up above the water and stand with no water clinging to it.

an10.81:2.14.pli: evamevaṁ kho, vāhana, imehi dasahi dhammehi tathāgato nissaṭo visaṁyutto vippamutto vimariyādīkatena cetasā viharatī”ti.
an10.81:2.14.eng: In the same way, the Realized One has escaped from ten things, so that he lives detached, liberated, with mind free of boundaries.”

an10.82:1.1.pli: Atha kho āyasmā ānando yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
an10.82:1.1.eng: Then Venerable Ānanda went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him:

an10.82:2.1.pli: “So vatānanda, bhikkhu ‘assaddho samāno imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissatī’ti netaṁ ṭhānaṁ vijjati.
an10.82:2.1.eng: “Ānanda, it is quite impossible for a faithless mendicant to achieve growth, improvement, or maturity in this teaching and training.

an10.82:3.1.pli: So vatānanda, bhikkhu ‘dussīlo samāno imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissatī’ti netaṁ ṭhānaṁ vijjati.
an10.82:3.1.eng: It is quite impossible for a mendicant who is unethical …

an10.82:4.1.pli: So vatānanda, bhikkhu ‘appassuto samāno imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissatī’ti netaṁ ṭhānaṁ vijjati.

an10.82:5.1.pli: So vatānanda, bhikkhu ‘dubbaco samāno imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissatī’ti netaṁ ṭhānaṁ vijjati.

an10.82:6.1.pli: So vatānanda, bhikkhu ‘pāpamitto samāno imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissatī’ti netaṁ ṭhānaṁ vijjati.

an10.82:7.1.pli: So vatānanda, bhikkhu ‘kusīto samāno imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissatī’ti netaṁ ṭhānaṁ vijjati.

an10.82:8.1.pli: So vatānanda, bhikkhu ‘muṭṭhassati samāno imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissatī’ti netaṁ ṭhānaṁ vijjati.

an10.82:9.1.pli: So vatānanda, bhikkhu ‘asantuṭṭho samāno imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissatī’ti netaṁ ṭhānaṁ vijjati.

an10.82:10.1.pli: So vatānanda, bhikkhu ‘pāpiccho samāno imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissatī’ti netaṁ ṭhānaṁ vijjati.

an10.82:11.1.pli: So vatānanda, bhikkhu ‘micchādiṭṭhiko samāno imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissatī’ti netaṁ ṭhānaṁ vijjati.
an10.82:11.1.eng: of wrong view to achieve growth, improvement, or maturity in this teaching and training.

an10.82:12.1.pli: So vatānanda, bhikkhu ‘imehi dasahi dhammehi samannāgato imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissatī’ti netaṁ ṭhānaṁ vijjati.
an10.82:12.1.eng: It is quite impossible for a mendicant with these ten qualities to achieve growth, improvement, or maturity in this teaching and training.

an10.82:13.1.pli: So vatānanda, bhikkhu ‘saddho samāno imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissatī’ti ṭhānametaṁ vijjati.
an10.82:13.1.eng: It is quite possible for a faithful mendicant to achieve growth, improvement, or maturity in this teaching and training.

an10.82:14.1.pli: So vatānanda, bhikkhu ‘sīlavā samāno imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissatī’ti ṭhānametaṁ vijjati.
an10.82:14.1.eng: It is quite possible for a mendicant who is ethical …

an10.82:15.1.pli: So vatānanda, bhikkhu ‘bahussuto sutadharo samāno imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissatī’ti ṭhānametaṁ vijjati.

an10.82:16.1.pli: So vatānanda, bhikkhu ‘suvaco samāno imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissatī’ti ṭhānametaṁ vijjati.

an10.82:17.1.pli: So vatānanda, bhikkhu ‘kalyāṇamitto samāno imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissatī’ti ṭhānametaṁ vijjati.

an10.82:18.1.pli: So vatānanda, bhikkhu ‘āraddhavīriyo samāno imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissatī’ti ṭhānametaṁ vijjati.

an10.82:19.1.pli: So vatānanda, bhikkhu ‘upaṭṭhitassati samāno imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissatī’ti ṭhānametaṁ vijjati.

an10.82:20.1.pli: So vatānanda, bhikkhu ‘santuṭṭho samāno imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissatī’ti ṭhānametaṁ vijjati.

an10.82:21.1.pli: So vatānanda, bhikkhu ‘appiccho samāno imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissatī’ti ṭhānametaṁ vijjati.

an10.82:22.1.pli: So vatānanda, bhikkhu ‘sammādiṭṭhiko samāno imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissatī’ti ṭhānametaṁ vijjati.
an10.82:22.1.eng: of right view to achieve growth, improvement, or maturity in this teaching and training.

an10.82:23.1.pli: So vatānanda, bhikkhu ‘imehi dasahi dhammehi samannāgato imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissatī’ti ṭhānametaṁ vijjatī”ti.
an10.82:23.1.eng: It is quite possible for a mendicant with these ten qualities to achieve growth, improvement, or maturity in this teaching and training.”
